topic: Someone finds out their medical records were mixed up with another patient's
raw_output:
[story 1]

The thick manila folder lay open on my desk, the crisp edges feeling suddenly sharp under my fingertips. I’d been reviewing my recent lab results, a routine check-in, when I noticed the discrepancy. The diagnosis codes, the medication history—they belonged to someone else. A cold knot tightened in my stomach, spreading outwards until my chest felt constricted. I reread the handwritten notes, the unfamiliar name staring back at me. My breath hitched, shallow and quick. I stood up abruptly, the chair scraping loudly against the linoleum floor. I needed air. The silence of the office suddenly amplified the frantic thumping in my ears.

[story 2]

It started as a misplaced file, a clerical error I was supposed to fix. Now, staring at the patient summary, a wave of nausea washed over me. Every line seemed to twist into an unrecognizable shape. I kept rubbing my temples, trying to force my focus back to the mundane task of filing, but my hands were trembling uncontrollably. I looked around the empty room, suddenly hyper-aware of every shadow. A low, ragged sound escaped my throat, a small, involuntary whimper. I sank back into my seat, my gaze fixed on the printed names, unable to look away, the air growing thick and heavy around me.

[story 3]

When the discrepancy became undeniable, a strange hollowness settled in my core. I picked up the documents, the paper feeling alien and wrong in my hands. My jaw clenched so tightly I could feel the muscles ache. I walked to the window, pulling the curtain aside, trying to focus on the distant traffic, but the noise just seemed to vibrate against my skull. I pressed the heels of my hands against my eyes, trying to physically block out the sight of the data. A slow, deliberate shake of my head signaled a profound sense of unease. I turned away from the desk, the movement stiff, and began pacing the small perimeter of the room, my footsteps echoing too loudly in the quiet space.
